title: refactor: use serde_json in doppler GET instead of custom navigator
description: doppler GET uses extract_path_field (secretx-core custom navigator) to avoid serde_json allocations. But resp.bytes() returns bytes::Bytes which is not zeroed on drop — the secret already sits in unzeroized heap before the navigator runs. The custom navigator only prevents additional allocations on top of an already-leaked copy. Replace with serde_json::from_slice. doppler already uses serde_json::json\! for PUT, so the dependency is already present. Also fix the misleading module doc claim that 'the response body is held in a Zeroizing buffer' — it is not; only the copy in SecretValue is zeroed.
